codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly routes nested foreign reads through the shared normalization seam and the new raw SQL regression covers the required string-form record-link case, but the feature still does not fully satisfy its own read-consistency proof because one acceptance regression uses a non-deterministic list_limit fixture.

issues.description: successful_foreign_save_matches_all_read_entrypoints calls list_limit(1) and then expects the only returned row to be save-return-root, but the fixture seeds just one row and does not establish any ordering contract showing why that row must be the single limited result. The feature contract and mission guidance require list_limit consistency assertions to use a uniquely identifiable fixture state rather than incidental ordering, so this test does not actually prove that list_limit returns the same hydrated value for the intended row.

summary: Fails scrutiny: the code change itself is directionally correct and scoped to the shared nested-foreign read normalization seam, but the feature's claimed all-entrypoint proof is incomplete because the list_limit acceptance regression is not deterministic for the target row.
